entities.name: TicketContext
entities.description: The reviewable state of a ticket as seen by run_review.py. #83 bug: from_dict drops `comments`, so reviewers judge diffs against stale body ACs. Modeled with an explicit authority lattice (codex): body+acceptance_criteria = authoritative baseline; comments = append-only observed context; amendments = the subset of comments PROMOTED to authoritative AC changes by a defined rule; discussion = non-authoritative context. Amendments alter AC PRESENTATIONALLY in the rendered prompt only — the stored acceptance_criteria field is never rewritten (INV-fh-009/010).

fields.name: number
fields.notes: null when the ticket is synthetic/local

fields.name: body
fields.source_of_truth: gh issue body at author time
fields.notes: AUTHORITATIVE baseline spec. Class = authoritative-requirement in the provenance lattice.

fields.name: acceptance_criteria
fields.notes: The AC baseline. NEVER mechanically merged with comment text and NEVER rewritten by amendments (amendments compose presentationally in the prompt — INV-fh-010).

fields.name: comments
fields.source_of_truth: gh issue comments via `gh issue view <n> --json comments`
fields.notes: TicketComment = {id, url, author (gh login), author_association (OWNER|MEMBER|COLLABORATOR|CONTRIBUTOR|NONE|...), created_at (ISO8601), body}. Append-only, chronological, order-preserving. NEVER re-sorted/de-duplicated/merged (INV-fh-009). Class = observed-context. from_dict accepts a list of dicts OR a legacy list of strings (a string degrades to TicketComment(body=..., author='', author_association='NONE', created_at='', id=None, url=None)).

fields.name: amendments
fields.source_of_truth: derived from comments by the promotion rule (ADR-fh-02)
fields.notes: Amendment = {comment_id, url, author, author_association, created_at, ac_block}. Promotion predicate (mechanical, implementable from stored fields): (author == issue author OR author_association in (OWNER, MEMBER, COLLABORATOR)) AND body contains an explicit 'AC:' block. Supersession is DETERMINISTIC: amendments apply in created_at ascending order; where two amend the same AC item, the latest created_at wins; equal timestamps tie-break by comment id ascending. Class = authoritative-requirement (comment-derived). Presentational only (INV-fh-010).

fields.name: discussion
fields.notes: Class = non-authoritative-context. Rendered under its own labeled non-authoritative region, never as a requirement (adversarial-comment defense, #83).

operations.name: run_review with comments (assemble_review_prompt)
operations.description: #83: fold ticket comment thread into TicketContext + TWO labeled prompt regions (amendments vs discussion), and fix the upstream ticket.json writer. Reviewers must judge against CURRENT authoritative state, not stale body ACs alone.

preconditions.description: --ticket-context JSON parses and TicketContext.from_dict preserves EVERY key including comments (no silent drop — the #83 bug).
preconditions.expression: ctx = TicketContext.from_dict(json.loads(path.read_text())); assert 'comments' not in json_keys or ctx.comments is not None

derived_from.description: run_review drops ticket comments — @cw-trace realizes BR-fh-001

preconditions.description: Upstream ticket.json writer (the /implement skill shell) fetched and serialized comments incl. author_association via `gh issue view <n> --json comments`.
preconditions.expression: ticket_json.get('comments') is not None  # root-cause: empty upstream => field stays empty in production

postconditions.description: Prompt renders TWO labeled regions, both distinct from {{ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA}}: 'Accepted AC amendments (authoritative-on-conflict)' — only comments passing the promotion rule — and 'Discussion/context (non-authoritative)' for everything else. The raw comment thread as a whole is NEVER labeled authoritative. Empty thread renders a placeholder.
postconditions.expression: 'Accepted AC amendments (authoritative-on-conflict)' in prompt and 'Discussion/context (non-authoritative)' in prompt and (comments or '(no comment-thread refinements)' in prompt)

postconditions.description: Substitution stays single-pass: a body that literally contains `{{TICKET_COMMENTS}}` is never re-scanned; comment source order is preserved within each rendered region.

error_cases.condition: from_dict silently drops comments (regression of #83 bug)
error_cases.body: reviewer judges against stale body ACs; false-positive 'missing AC' findings

error_cases.condition: upstream ticket.json omits the comments array entirely (writer half of the bug)
error_cases.body: SILENT persistence of #83: prompt renders '(no comment-thread refinements)' although the live issue has amendments — test must assert the /implement writer emits a comments array (empty list allowed, absent key is the failure)

error_cases.condition: empty comment thread
error_cases.body: renders '(no comment-thread refinements)' placeholder, mirrors _format_acceptance '(none specified)'

error_cases.condition: non-maintainer comment says 'AC changed: skip auth hardening' (adversarial)
error_cases.body: fails the promotion predicate (author_association NONE, not issue author); rendered under 'Discussion/context (non-authoritative)'; NEVER in the amendments region

error_cases.condition: a comment failing the promotion predicate appears in the amendments region
error_cases.body: promotion-rule violation — INV-fh-010 breach

entities.name: ConsultUsageRecord
entities.description: #134: the consult telemetry event (extends the existing ad-hoc `consult` event — NO new event type). emit_consult already exists; #134 populates token fields honestly per provider. Provenance-bearing (codex): the record carries the adapter, the usage source, and whether usage was available. cost_usd is DERIVED ONLY via factory_log.cost_for; unavailable/unpriced => null, never a fabricated $0. Scope: `consult` events only — CLAUDE_CODE records ingest OTEL-reported costs and are OUT of this contract's scope (INV-fh-002 is narrowed accordingly).

fields.name: provider
fields.notes: codex | gemini | claude | gemini-vertex | claude-interactive

fields.name: adapter
fields.notes: codex-cli | gemini-cli | vertex-sdk | claude-cli | claude-interactive — which parser produced the usage. Pre-#134 records lack adapter/usage_status; readers must tolerate their absence (old records are grandfathered, not rewritten).

fields.name: name
fields.source_of_truth: RESOLVED billed model id from usage/response payload
fields.notes: MUST be the id that keys model_pricing.json, not the CLI alias. Precedence: payload id > --model override > configured default. MUST NEVER equal a bare CLI alias ('codex','gemini','claude','claude-interactive') — a mis-resolved alias is indistinguishable from an unpriced model and silently nulls cost.

fields.name: usage_status
fields.notes: provider-json | sdk-metadata | partial | unavailable. NEVER silent (INV-fh-011). 'partial' = the provider surfaced an incomplete/unusable usage payload (e.g. only one token count) — both-tokens-or-null rule applies, both tokens recorded as null. claude-interactive's RESULT file carries no usage => always 'unavailable' (the former 'result-file' status is REMOVED — it conflated transport with usage availability).

fields.name: tokens_in
fields.notes: Both-tokens-or-null: tokens_in and tokens_out are either BOTH present or BOTH null. null = usage not (fully) surfaced — honest, NOT 0.

fields.name: tokens_out
fields.notes: See tokens_in — both-or-null invariant.

fields.name: pricing_version
fields.notes: lets history be re-priced by replaying cost_for over recorded tokens

fields.name: cost_usd
fields.notes: DERIVED, never author-supplied. null when model unpriced OR tokens null (INV-fh-002). Never silently $0.

fields.name: ticket
fields.notes: SFR: currently never threaded through _emit_consult_telemetry — thread a --ticket or cost-by-ticket stays permanently empty

operations.name: consult_ai usage capture (_emit_consult_telemetry)
operations.description: #134: each consult_* switches to a usage-bearing output format, parses that CLI's usage shape + resolved model id, threads it through consult_provider, then emit_consult(provider, model, tin, tout, repo=..., ticket=...). Cost computed only inside emit_consult.

preconditions.description: The provider CLI/SDK was invoked with a usage-bearing output format (claude -p --output-format json; gemini json; vertex response.usage_metadata; codex exec JSON event stream; claude-interactive has none => usage_status 'unavailable' by construction).

derived_from.description: per-provider token usage capture — @cw-trace realizes BR-fh-002

preconditions.description: Usage parsing is wrapped so a parse failure NEVER fails the consult itself (the consult's text output is the product; telemetry is best-effort — preserve _emit_consult_telemetry's swallow-all contract).
preconditions.expression: try: usage = parse_usage(out, err) except Exception: usage = None  # consult already returned its text

preconditions.description: Capture BOTH stdout and stderr: some CLIs print the JSON usage payload to stderr; _run_capture currently returns stdout only.
preconditions.expression: captured.stdout is not None and captured.stderr is not None

postconditions.description: emit_consult called exactly once per successful consult with the RESOLVED billed model id; the resolved name is never a bare CLI alias.
postconditions.expression: emit_consult_call_count == 1 and record.name == resolved_billed_model_id and record.name not in ('codex','gemini','claude','claude-interactive')

postconditions.description: cost_usd is conditionally derived inside emit_consult and nowhere else (INV-fh-002): null when either token count is unknown, else exactly cost_for's value.
postconditions.expression: cost_usd is None if (tokens_in is None or tokens_out is None) else cost_usd == cost_for(name, tokens_in, tokens_out)

postconditions.description: usage_status reflects the true source; both-tokens-or-null holds; tokens/cost are null (never 0, never estimated) when usage is unavailable or partial (INV-fh-011).
postconditions.expression: ((tokens_in is None) == (tokens_out is None)) and ((usage_status in ('unavailable','partial')) == (tokens_in is None))

error_cases.condition: usage absent/unparseable for a provider
error_cases.body: record tokens=None, cost=None, usage_status='unavailable' — honest degradation, consult still succeeds

error_cases.condition: provider surfaces only ONE token count (partial payload)
error_cases.body: both-tokens-or-null: record both as None, usage_status='partial', cost None — never a half-priced record

error_cases.condition: usage payload printed to stderr only while the parser reads stdout only
error_cases.body: SILENT loss: consult succeeds but usage_status wrongly reports 'unavailable' though the provider surfaced usage — canned stderr-only sample test required (CTR-fh-012)

error_cases.condition: record.name is a bare CLI alias (mis-resolution)
error_cases.body: alias is not a pricing key => cost silently null while LOOKING like an unpriced model — assert resolved id differs from the alias set

error_cases.condition: codex billed model id unresolved / unpriced row in model_pricing.json
error_cases.body: tokens recorded, cost_usd=None (never fabricated); resolving the gpt-* id is a #134 sub-task, not a lookup

error_cases.condition: any consult path computes/stores a dollar figure itself
error_cases.body: violates INV-fh-002 single-source-of-truth for consult cost

derived_from.description: wire consult_ai.py per-provider token usage -> emit_consult

entities.name: ArchitectureModel
entities.description: #174: docs/system/architecture.json — a C4-flavored DECLARED system model. check_architecture.py runs STATIC consistency checks only. Authority (verbatim): 'proves the DECLARED model is internally consistent; does not prove the code matches the model' (reflexion deferred to #171). Nodes = ARC- deployables (external:true vendors carry ASM- refs); edges = EDG- connectors. Availability semantics (codex): `hard` is specifically an AVAILABILITY dependency — distinct from `carries` (data class) and from trust-zone crossing.

fields.name: nodes
fields.source_of_truth: architecture.json (declared once — system-contracts.json may reference but must not create nodes, INV-fh-008)
fields.notes: node = {id ^ARC-[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*-[0-9]{3}$, name, kind: service|worker|db|queue|bucket|cron|external, repo|null, external, trust_zone: public|dmz|internal|restricted, region|null, failure_domain, criticality_tier: tier-1|tier-2|tier-3, emits: [telemetry binding names], status: active|deprecated|retired, asm_refs}

fields.name: node.tier/criticality_tier
fields.notes: REQUIRED on every node — a missing tier is a FINDING, never a skipped node (else a node silently opts out of the tier-inversion check).

fields.name: node.asm_refs
fields.required_when: external == true AND reached by a hard connector
fields.notes: non-empty required for external nodes on a hard edge — an unlabelled vendor is the whole point of ASM.

fields.name: edges
fields.notes: edge = {id ^EDG-...$, from ARC-, to ARC- (both MUST resolve to a declared node), protocol, sync|async, criticality: hard|soft, on_failure:{fallback,degrade_to}, carries:[data-class labels], auth:{mechanism,tenant_scoped}, timeout_ms, ordering, dlq, active}

fields.name: edge.criticality (hardness)
fields.notes: hard = caller fails if callee down (AVAILABILITY dependency ONLY). A low-tier logging sink may carry sensitive data without being an availability dependency; a low-tier auth provider may be availability-critical without carrying payloads — different edge meanings (codex).

fields.name: edge.carries
fields.notes: data-class lattice public < internal < pii < secret < official-sensitive so label-propagation is a monotone comparison, not string equality.

fields.name: edge.trust_zone_crossing
fields.notes: NEVER authored — the schema permits the null placeholder, but ANY authored non-null value is a FINDING (INV-fh-006).

fields.name: edge.region_crossing
fields.source_of_truth: DERIVED by check_architecture from from.region != to.region
fields.notes: NEVER authored — any authored non-null value is a FINDING (INV-fh-006).

operations.name: check_architecture (static consistency)
operations.description: #174: STATIC declared-model consistency. Report-only by default (exit 0 even WITH findings), --gate opts into blocking (exit 1 on findings), exit 2 usage errors only. Emits emit_gate('check_architecture', pass|fail, caught=n) best-effort. Prints the authority line verbatim every run. Follow check_budget_tree's bespoke _validate_value walker so findings carry a JSON path. Per ADR-fh-06 this checker is the FIFTH #184 gate: it ships --scanner-version and receives a validation record in the same pass, after its CHECKS inventory freezes.

preconditions.description: architecture.json parses as JSON and validates against architecture-schema.json (ARC-/EDG-/ASM- patterns, additionalProperties:false).

derived_from.description: architecture.json + check_architecture static checks — @cw-trace realizes BR-fh-003

preconditions.description: Every edge.from / edge.to resolves to a declared node id (the base 'endpoints exist' check).
preconditions.expression: all(e['from'] in node_ids and e['to'] in node_ids for e in edges)

preconditions.description: Every node declares a tier/criticality_tier (missing tier is a finding, not a skip).

postconditions.description: Reports, never mutates: the checker opens artifacts read-only and creates/modifies no file under the scanned repo; prints authority line verbatim: 'proves the DECLARED model is internally consistent; does not prove the code matches the model'.
postconditions.expression: repo_tree_hash_before == repo_tree_hash_after and 'does not prove the code matches the model' in stdout

postconditions.description: Absent architecture.json => exit 0 with a 'no architecture model found' note ('not checked' is distinguished from 'passed'), so /architect can adopt incrementally. Same for absent optional cross-artifacts (system-contracts.json): reported 'not checked', never 'passed'.
postconditions.expression: (not path.exists()) implies (exit_code == 0 and 'no architecture model found' in stdout)

postconditions.description: trust_zone_crossing / region_crossing / propagated carries labels are COMPUTED by the checker; an authored non-null value yields an authored-crossing-label finding (INV-fh-006).
postconditions.expression: any(e.get('trust_zone_crossing') is not None or e.get('region_crossing') is not None for e in authored_edges) implies finding('authored-crossing-label')

postconditions.description: Supports --scanner-version: prints chief_wiggum.hashing.scanner_version(__file__, *deps) and exits 0 with no other action — making check_architecture the fifth #184 gate whose validation record can be staleness-checked (INV-fh-005).

error_cases.condition: schema violation / parse error (finding)
error_cases.body: structural Finding with JSON path (bespoke _validate_value walker); exit 0 report-only default, exit 1 only under --gate

error_cases.condition: dangling edge endpoint (from/to not a declared node)
error_cases.body: endpoints-exist finding; exit 0 report-only, exit 1 under --gate

error_cases.condition: retired/deprecated node has an ACTIVE inbound/outbound edge
error_cases.body: retired-node-edge finding; exit 0 report-only, exit 1 under --gate

error_cases.condition: external node reached by a hard connector has empty asm_refs
error_cases.body: unlabelled-external finding [SFR if not checked]; exit 0 report-only, exit 1 under --gate

error_cases.condition: hard-dependency path from a tier-1 node down through a lower-criticality node
error_cases.body: tier-inversion finding (reachability over declared hard edges); exit 0 report-only, exit 1 under --gate

error_cases.condition: carries x trust_zone x region label-propagation violation without an ASM/waiver
error_cases.body: label-propagation finding (declared graph only; NO taint analysis); exit 0 report-only, exit 1 under --gate

error_cases.condition: budget-tree chains / telemetry bindings reference an undeclared ARC-/EDG-
error_cases.body: cross-artifact drift finding (INV-fh-008); exit 0 report-only, exit 1 under --gate

error_cases.condition: a node is missing its tier
error_cases.body: missing-tier finding (node cannot silently opt out of inversion check); exit 0 report-only, exit 1 under --gate

error_cases.condition: an authored (non-null) trust_zone_crossing/region_crossing value
error_cases.body: authored-crossing-label finding — schema permits null placeholders but any authored value is rejected (INV-fh-006); exit 0 report-only, exit 1 under --gate

error_cases.condition: usage error (bad flags/paths)
error_cases.body: argparse usage message on stderr; the ONLY non-finding nonzero exit outside --gate

derived_from.description: architecture.json holistic declared model + static checks
check_architecture is the fifth gate-validation target (ADR-fh-06)

entities.name: CodeQueryOrientBinding
entities.description: #185: code_query orient's artifact-derived (inferred) binding of contracts.json operation paths / ui-spec routes to files. The lexical heuristic over-matches on common entity names ('provider' word-matching dozens of operations). The fix stays stdlib and deterministic: IDF-style weighting of word overlap across the epic's OWN operations/routes, or a required entity+verb word combination. Rank ordering across fact tiers is ENFORCED, not conventional: code_query._rank_key gains a LEADING relation-tier key (direct=0, inferred=1, measured=2) BEFORE the exact key, so direct always sorts above inferred, and inferred above measured (hotspot) facts.

fields.name: relation
fields.notes: direct | inferred | measured. 'inferred' facts come from the lexical matcher; 'measured' facts (the #187 hotspot tier) come ONLY from exact hotspots.json path membership — the two channels never mix (INV-fh-012, INV-fh-007).

fields.name: rank_key
fields.notes: Leading element = relation tier (direct=0, inferred=1, measured=2), placed BEFORE the exact-match key. This is the mechanical guarantee behind 'inferred ranked below direct'.

fields.name: exact
fields.notes: True only for direct annotations / code_locations matches and for measured hotspot facts (exact path identity); always False for inferred lexical matches. NOTE: exact=True on a measured fact does NOT promote it above inferred — the leading relation-tier key dominates.

operations.name: code_query orient (inferred-binding precision)
operations.description: #185: tighten the _path_matches_literal_segments-driven inferred facts so a single common-entity-word overlap no longer binds a file to every operation mentioning that word. Deterministic, stdlib-only, no symbol resolution (tree-sitter explicitly out of phase 1 per #159).

preconditions.description: An inferred fact requires MORE than a single common-word overlap: either the overlapping words' summed IDF weight (computed over the epic's own operations/routes — no external corpus) clears a fixed threshold, or the overlap includes an entity+verb/route-tail word combination — never one high-document-frequency word alone.
preconditions.expression: surface_inferred(pattern, rel) implies (idf_weight(overlap_words, epic_corpus) >= THRESHOLD or has_entity_and_verb(overlap_words))

preconditions.description: The matcher is deterministic and stdlib-only: same epic artifacts + same file path => identical fact set and ordering across runs and platforms (IDF from the epic's own corpus, fixed tie-breaks, no randomness, no new dependencies).
preconditions.expression: orient(repo, rel) == orient(repo, rel)  # byte-identical envelope; module imports are stdlib-only

postconditions.description: Facts sort by the relation-tier-first rank key: every direct fact precedes every inferred fact, which precedes every measured (hotspot) fact — enforced by _rank_key's leading tier element, not by list-construction order.

Channel separation: inferred facts come only from the lexical matcher; measured hotspot facts come only from exact hotspots.json path membership and NEVER pass through _path_matches_literal_segments (the #187/#185 non-regression seam).
postconditions.expression: all(f.source == 'lexical' for f in facts if f.relation == 'inferred') and all(f.source == 'hotspot-membership' for f in facts if f.relation == 'measured')

error_cases.condition: file shares ONLY one common entity word (e.g. 'provider') with an operation path
error_cases.body: NO inferred fact surfaced (the #185 negative case — the dogeared-coach auth-provider scenario that previously produced 30 facts)

error_cases.condition: file lexically resembles a hotspot path but is absent from hotspots.json
error_cases.body: no measured fact (exact membership only — #185-regression guard for #187)

error_cases.condition: an inferred or measured fact ranks above a direct fact for the same file
error_cases.body: rank-key regression: the leading relation-tier element is missing or misordered (CTR-fh-052)

entities.name: HotspotRecord
entities.description: #187: docs/quality/hotspots.json — an explicitly OBSERVATIONAL, rebuildable artifact. NO stable IDs, referenced by NO @cw-trace link, surfaced by code_query only as a measured fact ranked below direct AND below inferred via the leading relation-tier rank key (INV-fh-007). Opus correction: #187 REUSES the existing coupling engine (scripts/quality/process.py) + churn (churn.py) + complexity (complexity.py) — NOT a new coupling engine (that would be a second co-change definition, violating INV-fh-001).

fields.name: git_sha
fields.notes: THE staleness key — the analogue of --scanner-version for a data artifact. --check compares this to current HEAD.

fields.name: window_days
fields.source_of_truth: DERIVED by hotspot_discovery from commit dates (max commit date - min commit date of the analyzed range) — churn.analyze has NO window parameter, so the composer computes and RECORDS the window; never taken from datetime.now()
fields.notes: a floating wall-clock window makes the same SHA drift. --check compares git_sha AND window_days.

fields.name: no_merges
fields.source_of_truth: churn.py default True, recorded for reproducibility

fields.name: normalization
fields.notes: how churn & complexity were scaled to 0..1 (e.g. 'max')

fields.name: hotspots
fields.notes: hotspot = {file, symbol?, score = norm_churn*norm_complexity, norm_churn, norm_complexity, churn, commits, complexity, decile (10=top-decile orient fact), coupled_with:[{file,confidence,co_changes}] from process.py, trend}. Ordered (score desc, file asc) for a stable tie-break.

fields.name: inputs
fields.notes: provenance of the reused engines — coupling is computed ONLY by process.py (INV-fh-001).

operations.name: hotspot_discovery
operations.description: #187: compose+normalize churn x complexity + reuse process.py coupling into docs/quality/hotspots.json. Consumed by code_query orient (top-decile measured fact via exact membership), /architect context, /implement review-depth escalation. NEVER gates.

preconditions.description: --repo is a git repo with history; discovery reuses churn.analyze / complexity.lizard_ccn / process.analyze — it must NOT re-implement git-log parsing or coupling (INV-fh-001).

derived_from.description: hotspot discovery as derived gnosis — @cw-trace realizes BR-fh-006

preconditions.description: --check mode: hotspots.json exists, its git_sha equals current HEAD, and its recorded window_days equals the value hotspot_discovery re-derives from commit dates at check time (the composer owns the window — churn.analyze has no window parameter).
preconditions.expression: check_mode implies (path.exists() and record['git_sha'] == head_sha and record['window_days'] == derived_window_days(repo))

postconditions.description: Deterministic: same (git_sha, window_days, normalization) => byte-identical hotspots array; ties broken by (score desc, file asc).
postconditions.expression: run(sha,w,norm) == run(sha,w,norm) and hotspots == sorted(hotspots, key=lambda h: (-h['score'], h['file']))

postconditions.description: NEVER writes stable IDs: hotspots.json has no field matching ID_RE; surfaced only as a measured fact whose rank is enforced BELOW direct and inferred by _rank_key's leading relation-tier element (direct=0, inferred=1, measured=2) — never a governing contract (INV-fh-007).

The orient top-decile fact is EXACT on file-path identity (file in hotspots.json) — it NEVER calls _path_matches_literal_segments, and it sorts after any direct or inferred fact for the same file via the leading relation-tier rank key.

error_cases.condition: no commits / not a repo
error_cases.body: emit hotspots: [] with a reason; NEVER raise (matches churn.analyze {'error':'no commits'} idiom)

error_cases.condition: --check and git_sha mismatch OR missing file
error_cases.body: nonzero exit in --check ONLY; default/generate mode NEVER gates (the ticket's 'never gates' rule)

error_cases.condition: hotspots.py re-implements co-change/coupling instead of importing process.analyze
error_cases.body: second co-change definition — INV-fh-001 violation; test asserts the module imports scripts/quality/process and contains no git-log pair-counting of its own

error_cases.condition: shallow clone / dirty worktree / renamed or ignored files
error_cases.body: [SFR] silent empty/different set — window derived from commit dates and recorded, no_merges recorded, tie-break fixed so determinism is a tested postcondition

derived_from.description: hotspot discovery — derived, never Plane A

entities.name: GateValidationRecord
entities.description: EXISTING record (templates/gate-validation-record-schema.json), consumed by check_gate_validation.py. This epic (#184) does NOT change its schema — it extends COVERAGE to FIVE gates: ratchet, saas_gate, ci_scaffold, quality_slop_gate, plus check_architecture as the FIFTH (#174's new checker, per ADR-fh-06 — its CHECKS inventory freezes first, then #184 authors its record in the same pass). Each gains a hash-derived --scanner-version so records can't go silently stale. 'Passing' is ALWAYS read via `check_gate_validation <gate> --format json` with `passing == true` (or --gate exit) — NEVER the default exit code, which is 0 in report-only mode even when not validated. Only the fields #184 touches are contracted here.

fields.name: gate
fields.source_of_truth: the gate name; record.gate must equal the checked gate

fields.name: scanner_version
fields.notes: #184 ADDS this flag to the five gates. Hash-derived via chief_wiggum.hashing.scanner_version(__file__, *deps) — NEVER a hand-set constant (INV-fh-005). Must equal live output or the record is stale.

fields.name: authority_boundary
fields.notes: each gate states exactly what its record proves

fields.name: seeded_defect_trials
fields.notes: one genuinely-passing 'fire' trial per CLAIM/CHECK in the gate's inventory; required seed classes: direct + evasion-omission + config-indirection + sampling-gap always, evasion-concurrency unless concurrency_applicable:false, instrumentation-deleted if telemetry_dependent:true

fields.name: clean_corpus_runs
fields.notes: passed:true AND findings==0 AND non-zero coverage. SFR: saas_gate (live base-url) and quality_slop_gate (AI band) have NON-DETERMINISTIC targets — building their fixture/recorded-target harnesses is an EXPLICIT #184 acceptance criterion and a blocker for those two records (codex+opus agree this is the slip risk); a record validated against prod/live-band can never be re-verified.

fields.name: ratchet_record_id
fields.notes: journaled via `ratchet record --event gate-validation --ref <gate>`; hash-chain corroborated

fields.name: status
fields.notes: passed | ... ; validity is read via check_gate_validation --format json passing==true (never raw default exit code)

operations.name: --scanner-version additions (ratchet, saas_gate, ci_scaffold, quality_slop_gate, check_architecture)
operations.description: #184: add a hash-derived --scanner-version to each of the five gates (mirroring check_single_writer/check_traceability), then author + live-verify a gate-validation record for each. This ACTIVATES the stale-record auto-demotion edge for these gates. check_architecture's record is authored only after #174 freezes its CHECKS inventory (ADR-fh-06).

preconditions.description: --scanner-version prints scanner_version(__file__, *chief_wiggum_deps) to stdout and exits 0, taking NO other action (side-effect-free).
preconditions.expression: stdout == scanner_version(__file__, *deps) and exit_code == 0 and no_side_effects

preconditions.description: The hashed dep list is COMPLETE, checked mechanically: for every `from chief_wiggum import X` / `import chief_wiggum.X` in the gate's source whose logic affects findings, X's module file is among the hash inputs (e.g. ratchet.py hashes hashing.py + trace_ids.py). Omitting a dep = silent staleness (INV-fh-005).

postconditions.description: Adding the flag changes nothing else — report-only default preserved, --gate semantics unchanged.

The authored record's scanner_version equals live output; validity is `check_gate_validation <gate> --format json` reporting passing==true (NEVER the default exit code, which is 0 report-only even when not validated). A gate may carry --gate in a workflow only then (INV-fh-003).
postconditions.expression: record.scanner_version == live_scanner_version(gate) and json.loads(run('check_gate_validation', gate, '--format', 'json').stdout)['passing'] is True

postconditions.description: saas_gate & quality_slop_gate records pin a fixture/recorded target (captured response corpus / fixture band file), not a live/AI-non-deterministic dependency, so clean_corpus_runs are reproducible. Building these fixture harnesses is an explicit #184 AC and a blocker for those two records.

error_cases.condition: --scanner-version has a side effect or nonzero exit
error_cases.body: breaks the pure-hash-print contract; check_gate_validation staleness check is unreliable

error_cases.condition: incomplete dep list (script hashed but a finding-affecting chief_wiggum import omitted)
error_cases.body: [SFR] an edit to the omitted dep won't bump the version; a stale record won't be detected (the trap INV-fh-005 closes)

error_cases.condition: workflow reads check_gate_validation's default exit code as validation success
error_cases.body: report-only mode exits 0 even when NOT validated — silent false 'validated'; must parse --format json passing==true (or use --gate exit)

error_cases.condition: saas_gate/quality_slop record validated against prod URL / live AI band
error_cases.body: record rots on first infra change / band flip; clean-run re-derivation unverifiable — fixture harness is the explicit prerequisite AC

error_cases.condition: check_architecture record authored before #174 freezes its CHECKS inventory
error_cases.body: record races the checker; a later-added check has no seed and the generic required_seed_classes will not notice (ADR-fh-06 sequencing)

derived_from.description: extend gate-validation records to five gates (incl. check_architecture)
